Home / Churn Rate Calculator

Churn Rate Calculator

Analyze customer churn: monthly and annual rates, revenue impact, customer lifetime, and how reducing churn saves you money.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your customer count at the beginning of the month.
2
Enter how many customers cancelled or failed to renew.
3
Enter how many new customers you acquired.
4
Enter your average revenue per customer to see revenue impact.
5
Check the churn snapshot for your monthly and annual rates.
6
See how much of your growth is eaten by churn in the Growth vs Churn section.
7
Review customer LTV and the annual cost of churn.
8
Explore the Reduce Churn scenarios to see how much you could save.
9
tools.solopreneur-churn-rate-calculator.how_to_use.8

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good monthly churn rate for SaaS?

Under 3% monthly is good for SMB SaaS, under 1-2% is great. Enterprise SaaS should target under 1%. Annual churn matters more — 3% monthly compounds to ~31% annual, meaning you lose a third of customers every year. The best SaaS companies keep monthly churn under 1%.

What is the difference between customer churn and revenue churn?

Customer churn measures the percentage of customers who leave. Revenue churn measures the percentage of revenue lost. Revenue churn can be higher if high-paying customers leave, or lower if only low-tier customers churn. Track both — they tell different stories.

How do I reduce churn quickly?

Fix onboarding first — most churn happens in the first 30 days. Add a welcome sequence, in-app guidance, and check-in emails at day 7 and 30. Second, implement dunning (retry failed payments). Involuntary churn from expired credit cards is 20-40% of total churn and is the easiest to fix.

How is customer lifetime calculated from churn?

Average lifetime = 1 / monthly churn rate. At 3% monthly churn, the average customer stays 1/0.03 ≈ 33 months. This is the standard SaaS formula. Some customers stay much longer, others churn immediately — it is a statistical average.

Why focus on churn instead of just acquiring more?

Reducing churn compounds: every retained customer generates revenue month after month without additional acquisition cost. Going from 5% to 3% churn increases average lifetime by 67% (from 20 to 33 months). Meanwhile, acquisition costs 5-7x more than retention. Fix the leaky bucket before pouring more water in.

Results

Copy All
Example

📉 Logo & Revenue Churn Analysis

• Customers at Start: 500 • New Customers Added: 25 • Customers Lost: 15 • Customers at End: 510 • Net Growth: +10 (2.0%) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 👥 Logo Churn (Customer Headcount) • Monthly Logo Churn: 3.0% • Annual Logo Churn: 30.6% 💰 Revenue Churn • Gross Revenue Churn (before expansion): 3.0% • Net Revenue Churn (after expansion): -0.2% • Monthly Revenue Lost to Churn: $750 • Annual Revenue Lost to Churn: $9,000 📈 GRR / NRR • GRR = 97.0% — 🟢 Healthy • NRR = 100.2% — 🟡 Positive ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 🟡 AVERAGE: 2-5% monthly logo churn is typical for SaaS. There is room for improvement. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 🔍 Churn Attribution (15 customers lost) • Voluntary (~60%): ~9 customers — fix with better onboarding • Involuntary (~40%): ~6 customers — fix with dunning emails
